sections.title: Already Usable Now
sections.body: The report layer can already narrate basin-context readiness using the two collected PDFs, the confirmed USGS 02193900 endpoint metadata, the downloaded USGS iv/dv JSON artifacts, and WQP station metadata for USGS-02196560.

sections.title: First Hydrology or Context Figure Dependency
sections.body: The first low-risk hydrology/context figure should use USGS site 02193900 daily and instantaneous reservoir-elevation and weather variables as the structured core, with the two basin PDFs as static watershed context. It must not claim inflow, outflow, or residence time yet.

sections.title: Placeholder Sections Kept Open
sections.body: Operational inflow forecasts, broader surface-water systems context, and additional USACE basin documentation remain placeholders because the weather.gov and USACE/DES sources are still blocked in this collection cycle.

report_subtitle: Partial context based on current collected artifacts

source_note.text: Current partial report context is grounded in local collected artifacts only: two contextual PDFs, USGS 02193900 endpoint metadata plus iv/dv JSON downloads, and WQP station metadata for USGS-02196560. Blocked sources are explicitly left as placeholders.

context_summary: This partial handoff covers source readiness, currently usable contextual assets, and the first hydrology/context figure dependencies. It does not claim complete operations, inflow, or full water-quality coverage.

metrics.label: Partial sources
metrics.note: WQP station metadata collected; result export still pending

figures.summary: Current usable assets include two watershed-context PDFs, USGS 02193900 structured iv/dv JSON downloads, and WQP station metadata for USGS-02196560.
figures.interpretation: The report can already describe source readiness and site coverage honestly, but it should not present a full reservoir-operations narrative yet because inflow and broader USACE operational sources remain incomplete.
figures.title: Current Clarks Hill data readiness
figures.highlight: Structured USGS reservoir-level and weather observations are now available for the first analytical pass.

figures.summary: A first hydrology/context figure can be built from USGS 02193900 daily and instantaneous records, supplemented by watershed context from the collected PDFs.
figures.interpretation: This figure must stay scoped to reservoir elevation and local meteorological covariates at site 02193900 until inflow, outflow, and richer water-quality exports are collected.
figures.title: First hydrology-context figure prerequisites
figures.highlight: Safe first figure scope: reservoir elevation plus local weather context, not full hydrodynamics.

figures.summary: Three high-value sources remain blocked in the current cycle: NWS inflows/forecasts, DES surface-water context, and the USACE Thurmond basin document.
figures.interpretation: These sections should remain placeholders in the HTML layer so the report can signal what is still missing without fabricating continuity in the operations story.
figures.title: Blocked source placeholders
figures.highlight: Inflow and broader operational context remain blocked on collection.
